rounds.name: Round 1 · 连接词
rounds.description: 转折 / 并列 / 因果 / 对比 / 让步 / 总结

tokens.v: A tax on plane tickets sounds like a clean idea — polluters pay, green technology gets funded —
whether it actually helps depends fully on how the tax is designed. I would support it only in a narrow form: place it on short routes where trains are already an option,
require by law that the money goes into cleaner transport. A flat tax on every ticket would miss the target on all three fronts.
the plan only works
passengers can switch. On routes like Paris–Lyon, a small extra fee pushes travelers onto high-speed rail
real emissions drop. On long-haul flights,
no train exists, the same tax just becomes a fixed cost. The flight still takes off,
the bill falls on economy passengers
business travelers feel nothing.
the scale is too small. Flying makes up only about 2.5% of global carbon; forcing airlines to use cleaner fuel
join a wider carbon-trading system would cover far more ground.
in several European trials, green-tax money was quietly moved into the general budget.
legal rules locking the money to green use, this is a revenue plan with a green label.

rounds.name: Round 4 · 句法骨架
rounds.description: 整块从句 / 介词短语 / 修饰结构

tokens.v: A tax on plane tickets sounds like a clean idea — polluters pay, green technology gets funded — but

tokens.answer: whether it actually helps depends fully on how the tax is designed.

tokens.v: I would support it only in a narrow form:

tokens.answer: place it on short routes where trains are already an option,

tokens.v:
and require by law that the money goes into cleaner transport.
First, the plan only works
On routes like Paris–Lyon or Beijing–Tianjin, a small extra fee pushes travelers onto high-speed rail and real emissions drop. On long-haul flights,
the same tax just becomes a fixed cost. The flight still takes off, and the bill falls on economy passengers

tokens.answer: while business travelers, whose tickets are paid by the company, feel nothing.

tokens.v: Second, the scale is too small. Flying makes up only about 2.5% of global carbon;

tokens.answer: forcing airlines to use cleaner fuel or join a wider carbon-trading system

tokens.v:
would cover far more ground.
Third, in several European trials, green-tax money was quietly moved into the general budget.

tokens.answer: Without legal rules locking the money to green use,

tokens.v: this is a revenue plan with a green label.
